question: Quel niveau de discipline de spec/prompt adopter pour deleguer des taches a un agent IA ?

principles.text: Adopter une discipline spec-first : plan-then-code (Claude Code plan mode via `/plan` et `--permission-mode plan`, Aider `/architect` mode), acceptance criteria explicites dans les issues, spec-driven development avec outils formels (GitHub Spec-Kit avec constitution.md, Amazon Kiro). Les evidences empiriques (TiCoder, TGen) montrent +12 a +46pp d'amelioration en pass@1 vs freeform prompting sur benchmarks synthetiques courts (MBPP, HumanEval). Indirectness a noter : ces gains ne sont pas encore valides sur du real-world feature work longer-horizon.

sources.name: GitHub Spec-Kit - spec-driven.md
sources.quote: Specifications don't serve code—code serves specifications

sources.name: GitHub Spec-Kit constitution.md Article III - Test-First Imperative
sources.quote: No implementation code shall be written before: 1. Unit tests are written 2. Tests are validated and approved by the user

sources.name: Naik et al. 2024 - TiCoder: LLM-Based Test-Driven Interactive Code Generation (arXiv:2404.10100, TSE 2024)
sources.quote: We observe an average absolute improvement of 45.97% in the pass@1 code generation accuracy for both datasets and across all LLMs within 5 user interactions

sources.name: Mathews & Nadi 2024 - TGen: Test-Driven Development for Code Generation (arXiv:2402.13521, 2 LLMs tested)
sources.quote: including tests contributes to solving an additional 12.0% and 8.5% of problems

sources.name: Fowler/Bird - Understanding Spec-Driven Development: Kiro, spec-kit, Tessl
ISO/IEC 5338:2023 §6.4.2 Stakeholder needs + §6.4.3 System requirements (Technical processes)

etd_balance.risques: Cout upfront spec, rigidite vs exploration
